noveltyJustification: All 3 findings are net-new for A4 (no prior A4 coverage). F-A4-01 (job-worker reopen-after-close) was NOT in the charter — surfaced by reading the file-watcher drain rationale and finding the unguarded twin. Charter's headline A4 P0 was refuted (real new knowledge, not a re-statement).

findingDetails.title: Ingest job worker not drained/fenced on shutdown — can reopen DB after close_db() and defeat at-rest WAL/clean-marker guarantee
findingDetails.evidence: fileWatcher drained first because get_db reopens closed DB (comment 1578-1585); job worker is fire-and-forget setImmediate(void drainQueue) (746-752) with no shuttingDown guard and absent from cleanup list (1563-1610); processFile->indexSingleFile->get_db()->initialize_db reopens + rewrites .unclean-shutdown marker (1562) leaving non-empty WAL at rest.
findingDetails.recommendation: Stop/await the ingest worker in fatalShutdown BEFORE vectorIndex.closeDb (mirror the fileWatcher drain), or have indexSingleFile use try_get_db() so it cannot reopen a closed DB; add a shuttingDown guard to drainQueue.
findingDetails.scopeProof: All cited lines inside A4 declared targets.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Drop to P2 if a supervisor/boot path stops the ingest worker before closeDb (none found this iter), or jobs idle at shutdown in practice.
findingDetails.mitigant: Durable crash recovery (resetIncompleteJobsToQueued, job-queue.ts:763-768) + boot unclean-marker repair (context-server.ts:376-426) make this recoverable, not data-loss — caps at P1.

findingDetails.title: Two parallel divergent SIGTERM/SIGINT handler stacks — inconsistent process exit code on signal
findingDetails.evidence: shutdown-hooks.ts installs its own process.once SIGTERM/SIGINT -> exit(143|130); context-server installs process.on SIGTERM/SIGINT -> fatalShutdown -> exit(0). Both fire; re-entrancy guards (shuttingDown 1559, running 63) prevent hook double-run but exit code is non-deterministic.
findingDetails.recommendation: Consolidate signal handling to one stack; have fatalShutdown own the exit code (use 143/130 to match convention) or have shutdown-hooks defer to the daemon path.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Both files are A4 targets; lines cited verbatim.

findingDetails.title: startIpcSocketServer not re-entrant — module-global activeServer/activeSockets overwritten without disposing prior server
findingDetails.evidence: activeServer/activeSocketPath set unconditionally at 357-358 each start; a second start in-process leaks the prior net.Server (only the current caller gets a close()). Returned close() does guard activeServer===server (372-375) but no start-time guard. Parent-dir-fsync-on-unlink absent but restart safely reclaims via canUnlinkExistingSocket (147-179) + bind unlink (335-346) so no permanent EADDRINUSE.
findingDetails.recommendation: Guard at start: if activeServer is non-null, dispose it (close + unlink) before overwriting, or refuse re-entrant start. Treat dir-fsync as a separate durability nicety.
findingDetails.scopeProof: socket-server.ts is an A4 target; lines cited verbatim.

traceabilityChecks: A4 target files confirmed in diff range via git diff --name-only
iteration-number discrepancy (JSONL=2 iterations, dispatch=5) expected under parallel execution; honoring dispatch per parallel-safety contract
charter A4 P0/socket hypotheses tested directly against close_db and drain order
integrationEvidence: context-server.ts:1607-1609 calls runShutdownHooks() from lib/runtime/shutdown-hooks.ts; two signal-handler stacks both observe SIGTERM/SIGINT (F-A4-02)
edgeCases: closeDb is synchronous and runs to completion once reached; 5000ms SHUTDOWN_DEADLINE only bounds awaited drains before it (slow-drain tail risk worsened by F-A4-01)
checkpointAllWal periodic (300s, unref) is a no-op if !db; it is a backstop not the shutdown guarantee — final TRUNCATE in close_db is the guarantee
TCP-fallback close() never unlinks (correct, no node); only unix path unlinks
concurrent-session checkout makes F-A4-01 window operationally reachable
ruledOut: Charter A4 P0 'no final WAL checkpoint on shutdown' REFUTED — close_db does explicit wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) on all connections before close (vector-index-store.ts:1644-1664)

Charter A4 'missing parent-dir fsync -> permanent stale-socket EADDRINUSE on restart' DOWNGRADED — ownership-fenced canUnlinkExistingSocket + bind-time unlink reclaim a stale node safely (147-179, 335-346)
confirmedCleanSurfaces: Final WAL checkpoint on shutdown PRESENT (close_db TRUNCATE all conns + marker removed only after confirmed checkpoint, 1623-1664)
Shutdown-hook timeout enforcement correct (per-hook Promise.race + unref timer + finally clear; re-entrancy guarded, shutdown-hooks.ts:95-118,62-77)
Socket close() idempotency for stale-instance case (activeServer===server guard, 372-375; ENOENT-tolerant unlink)
selfCheck: P0 candidate (F-A4-01) ran Hunter/Skeptic/Referee in-iteration: Hunter flagged reopen-after-close; Skeptic asked whether crash recovery erases the window; Referee ruled window real but recoverable -> P1 not P0. Headline charter P0 adversarially refuted via close_db source read.
durationNote: single iteration, verify profile, 12 tool calls
